reviewed: STANDING METHOD RULE 7: SHARED-MANIFEST WRITE LOCK
STANDING METHOD RULE 8: SUITE MANIFEST GUARD
Only their interaction with committed rule 5 attribution, committed rule 6 immutable-revision evidence, file-disjoint dispatch, BEST-STACK DELTA, and the structured-report requirement
not_reopened: Round 1 findings and attribution spot checks

evidence: SKILL.md:81-86 gives the named shared manifests one coordinator-granted, fleet-wide active writer and requires manifest-editing lanes to declare that scope at dispatch.
The lock strengthens the committed file-disjoint rule at SKILL.md:12-15 and is compatible with the mandatory BEST-STACK DELTA declaration at SKILL.md:114-118.
Grant-and-release wording makes the authorization lifecycle explicit; it does not grant lanes implicit write authority.
SKILL.md:87-89 requires start and end sha256 values for every governing shared manifest on any wide-suite run cited as evidence.
A mismatch invalidates the measurement and requires a rerun; it is therefore not booked as a test red and never enters rule 5 failure attribution.

Rule 5 at SKILL.md:62-73 applies to failures from a valid wide-suite measurement. Rule 8 rejects a manifest-contaminated measurement before any failure ruling, so its no-attribution instruction does not bypass rule 5.
On the required stable rerun, any remaining out-of-lane failures still require rule 5 attribution and the report-schema honesty constraints still apply.
Rule 6 at SKILL.md:74-80 controls evidence grade and execution location; rule 8 adds an orthogonal input-stability check.
Promotion evidence must satisfy both immutable-revision execution and matching manifest hashes. Working-tree iteration runs remain iteration-only even when their hashes match.
The SKILL.md diff from bd9fb1eb is exactly +9/-0 and contains only rules 7-8.
git diff --check passed for the SKILL.md delta and the Round 4 spec addition.
No new contradiction was found within the bounded interaction surface.

verdict_rationale: Rules 7-8 are operationally clear and compose correctly with the committed evidence hierarchy. Rule 7 prevents concurrent shared-manifest writers; rule 8 rejects a run whose governing manifest changed before rule 5 attribution or rule 6 evidence grading can be applied. The stable rerun remains fully subject to rules 5-6 and the report schema. No blocker remains in the bounded Round 4 delta.

commit_authorization.reason: Authorize the two-rule skill delta, its bounded Round 4 dispatch record, and the two deciding review records. No other dirty-worktree path is authorized.
excluded: All lane logs and pid files

suite_record.reason: Round 4 is a bounded governance micro-review; the prior suite and passed items were not reopened.

honest_issues: The historical shared-working-tree suite remains red and iteration-only; this ruling is not promotion evidence.
